content: When you send a message online, whether it's an email, a text, or a message on a social media platform, you want to make sure that only the intended recipient can read it. That's where encryption comes in. Encryption is like a secret code that scrambles your message so that only the person with the right "key" can unscramble it and read it. When you send an encrypted message, it gets converted into a jumbled mix of letters and numbers that looks like gibberish to anyone who tries to intercept it. But when the recipient gets the message, their device uses the key to unscramble it, and they can read it just like you wrote it. This way, even if someone tries to hack into your message, all they'll see is the jumbled code, keeping your private messages private.
